data.title: Wall Street ends in the red as retail inflation comes in slightly cooler, earnings fail to lift sentiment
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages finished lower on Tuesday, as investors assessed cooler-than-expected core CPI inflation and some important earnings reports.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last -0.2% in afternoon trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.1%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.8%.

“Sellers sent the major indices lower in the morning. By noon, buyers lifted the index, erasing earlier losses, only for sellers to take the index slightly lower. Geopolitically, Trump’s ‘Don-roe Doctrine’ is in focus relating to Venezuela and Iran. This gave energy firms like ExxonMobil (XOM) and Chevron (CVX) a lift today,” Chris Lau, Investing Group Leader for DIY Value Investing, told Seeking Alpha.

“Overall, stock markets could not shake off two headwinds: inflation and credit card regulation. Trump did not want consumers to face higher electricity bills from AI data centers in the former, and he wanted a 10% cap on swipe fees in the latter case,” Lau added.

The December Consumer Price Index came in at +0.3% MoM vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.2% prior, +2.7% YoY vs. +2.6% consensus and +2.7% prior.

Core CPI (excludes food and energy) came in at +0.2% MoM vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.2% prior, +2.6% YoY vs. +2.7% consensus and +2.6% prior.

In other news, President Donald Trump said he'll announce his nomination for the Federal Reserve chair within "the next few weeks," as the Justice Department continues probe into current Fed Chair Jerome Powell.

Also, new home sales slipped 0.1% MoM to 737K in October, vs. 716K expected, from 738K in the prior month, according to the U.S. Census Bureau.

Among companies reporting on Tuesday, JPMorgan Chase (JPM) -4% posted better-than-expected earnings, while Delta Air Lines (DAL) -2.4% had year-over-year revenue growth. 

Big companies reporting this week include Bank of America (BAC), Goldman Sachs (GS), and Citigroup (C).

Over in the bond market, the benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was flat at 4.18%, while the U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) was 1 basis point lower to 3.53%. A 30-Year bond auction traded through.

data.title: Park Aerospace GAAP EPS of $0.15, revenue of $17.33M
data.description:
• Park Aerospace press release (PKE): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.15.
  • Revenue of $17.33M.

  • Adjusted EBITDA for the 2026 fiscal year third quarter was $4,226,000 compared to $2,415,000 for the 2025 fiscal year third quarter and $3,401,000 for the 2026 fiscal year second quarter. Adjusted EBITDA for the current fiscal year’s first nine months was $10,590,000 compared to $8,231,000 for last fiscal year’s first nine months.

data.title: Netflix considering changing bid for Warner Bros. to all cash - report
data.description:
Netflix (NFLX) is working on revised terms for it Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) purchase and is considering making its offer all cash for the company's studios and streaming businesses.

The changes are part of an effort to speed up a sale that's expected to take months to close and comes as rival bidder Paramount Skydance (PSKY) has offered an all-cash $30 a share, according to a Bloomberg report on Tuesday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

Warner Bros. rose 1.1%, while Netflix gained 1.3%.

Developing story ...

data.title: Burford Capital announces pricing and upsizing of private offering of senior notes
data.description:
• Burford Capital (BUR) on Tuesday announced the pricing of its private offering of $500 million aggregate principal amount of 8.500% senior notes due 2034 by its indirect, wholly owned subsidiary, Burford Capital Global Finance, which represents an increase from the previously announced offering size.
  • The offering is expected to close on January 15, 2026.
  • Burford Capital intends to use the net proceeds from the offering of the Securities to redeem as soon as practicable following the closing of the offering the 5.000% bonds due 2026 of Burford Capital and the remainder for general corporate purposes, which may include the repayment or retirement of other existing indebtedness.
  • BUR is -0.72% to $9.57.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Kodiak Gas Services raised at Barclays, as execution plus value equals opportunity
data.description:
Kodiak Gas Services (KGS) +2.6% in Tuesday's trading as Barclays upgraded the operator of contract compression infrastructure for oil and gas industry customers to Overweight from Equal Weight with a $42 price target, raised from $35, citing a positive growth outlook, strong execution track record, and discounted valuation.

Kodiak (KGS) has a strong fundamental story in its asset composition and geographical reach as well as industry-leading mechanical uptime and fleet utilization, and continues to see tailwinds from a resilient demand outlook for compression services amid supply tightness with extended lead times for new orders, Barclays analyst Theresa Chen said.

Compression remains a key part of the value chain that enables gas to move efficiently from the wellhead to end markets, but extended lead times of 80 or more weeks for new compression equipment have created tightness between supply and demand, and Chen believes this constrained supply underpins steady and durable demand as well as pricing power for Kodiak's (KGS) compression services, which are essential in helping producers bring gas volumes to market reliably and efficiently.

EQT's (EQT) full exit in December has removed any risk related to sponsor overhang, Chen said, expecting greater investor receptivity for Kodiak (KGS) in 2026 with large sell-downs no longer on the horizon, and the stock should trade more in line with its fundamental profile, with one less key risk.

data.title: Unity Bancorp GAAP EPS of $1.52
data.description:
• Unity Bancorp press release (UNTY): Q4 GAAP EPS of $1.52.

data.title: 2026 will bring a recession, Dow retracing 15-20% – Ariel Investments’ John Rogers
data.description:
The U.S. economy will likely slide into a small recession by the end of 2026, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) dropping 15% to 20%, according to John Rogers, chairman and co-chief executive officer of Ariel Investments.

Rogers made the prediction at the Executives’ Club of Chicago’s annual outlook event, warning that average-income consumers are struggling with high living costs despite wealthy Americans continuing to spend freely.

Rogers explained that the contrast between different income groups is driving his bearish outlook.

“Wealthy consumers are doing really great, going on cruises, going to Vegas, going on and spending money on all kinds of exciting things. But the average consumer is really struggling,” he said.

The veteran fund manager also expressed concern about the stock market’s heavy concentration in big technology stocks (MAGS), (MAGX), noting that the AI boom has created a disconnect between Wall Street and the broader economy.

Not all economists share Rogers’ recession forecast. Diane Swonk, chief economist at KPMG, predicted at the same event that the U.S. will dodge a recession, though she expects the Dow (DJI) to end the year lower at 43,000, down from nearly 49,200 currently. She expects the Federal Reserve to cut rates three times this year as pressure mounts on policymakers to ease borrowing costs.

Swonk cautioned that inflation could remain sticky due to large tax refunds flowing to wealthy Americans early in the year and minimum wage increases in several states, according to the economist. Despite these headwinds, she said investors will continue to flock to gold as a safe haven.

Rogers also said he continues to bet on small cap companies, citing Smucker Co. (SJM) as a stock that performs well during tough economic times.

Dow-tracking ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (UDOW), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW)

data.title: UberEats and DoorDash allegedly blocked tipping for delivery drivers
data.description:
A New York City Department of Consumer and Worker Protection ((DWCP)) report alleges that Uber Eats (UBER) and DoorDash (DASH) designed their customer interfaces in ways that discouraged tipping, resulting in more than $550 million in lost gratuities for delivery drivers.

“Our report blows the whistle on a massive scheme by Uber and DoorDash to drive down worker pay by more than $550M,” said DCWP Commissioner Samuel A. A. Levine.

Under new worker protection laws regarding tips, third-party food and grocery delivery apps must offer a 10% tip option and allow tipping before or during order placement. Employers must also ensure timely pay of tips (within 7 days), and are prohibited from keeping gratuities or using tip credits if workers spend significant time on non-tipped duties.

“If companies do not follow new tipping laws going into effect later this month, they will face significant consequences,” Levine warned.

According to the report, after DCWP began enforcing the delivery worker minimum pay rate in December 2023, Uber (UBER) and DoorDash (DASH) designed changes to their interface which made it more difficult to leave tips for delivery drivers.The study found that tipping decreased immediately after Uber Eats (UBER) and DoorDash (DASH) made these changes, declining to just $0.76 per delivery compared to an average tip of $2.17 per delivery on restaurant delivery apps that offer a tipping option at checkout.

The changes have resulted in a $554M decrease in Uber Eats and DoorDash (DASH) workers’ tips, or approximately $5,800 per worker per year.

UberEats (UBER) and DoorDash (DASH) have not yet responded to Seeking Alpha’s request for a comment.

data.title: Warren says U.S. reserve fund shouldn't be used to boost Venezuela investors
data.description:
Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-MA) has urged the U.S. Treasury Department against using the Exchange Stabilization Fund ("ESF") to support financial institutions considering investments in Venezuela, arguing it shouldn't be used to enrich hedge funds, according to a media report on Tuesday.

The news comes after the arrest and capture of Venezuela's president by U.S. forces early this month.

Established in the 1930s, the ESF is a Treasury reserve fund used to help stabilize financial markets and currencies. It was used in 2025 to a secure a multibillion-dollar swap line with Argentina. 

“President Trump should not launch illegal foreign wars or deploy taxpayer resources to facilitate Wall Street war profiteering, especially as his economic policies are failing here at home,” Warren, the ranking member of the Senate Committee on Banking, and Urban Affairs, wrote in the letter to Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, Bloomberg reported.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: SA analysts see bullish year ahead for S&P 500
data.description:
Seeking Alpha analysts are overwhelmingly bullish that the S&P 500 (SP500) (SPY) will gain more than 5% this year, according to a recent survey.

Of the 97 analysts polled, 73.2% saw the S&P climbing more than 5% in 2026, while 14.4% expected the index to slide more than 5%, and 11.3% didn't see it rising or falling more than 5%.

The average analyst target for the index was 7,432, with a median estimate of 7,553. SA analysts Andrew Hecht and Geneva Investor had the highest target prediction, with both forecasting 10,000, while Damir Tokic had the lowest at 4,100.

Of the 71 analysts who indicated they were bullish about the index's performance this year, eight said they expected the S&P 500 to soar 20% or more in 2026, while 17 predicted a jump of 15% to 20%. Another 25, or 25.8%, were moderately bullish, forecasting a gain of 10% to 15%, with an additional 22 expecting an increase of 5% to 10%.

As for the bears, two of the 97 analysts polled expected the index to dive 25% or more, while another two saw the index dropping 15% to 20%. Four of the analysts saw the index sliding 10% to 15%, while six expected it to dip 5% to 10%.
